task_completion.description: Log into the OpenClinica web interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenClinica dashboard or home page is visible, indicating a successful login (expected user: root).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the records for subject DM-101
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Subject Matrix or View Subject page is open, specifically displaying the study subject 'DM-101'.

task_completion.description: Access the audit history for the 'Systolic BP' field
task_completion.visual_evidence: An OpenClinica Audit Log window, popup, or page is visible for the 'Systolic BP' item within the 'Vital Signs' CRF of the Baseline Assessment.

task_completion.description: Create the audit findings text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the file '/home/ga/Documents/audit_findings.txt' being created or opened.

task_completion.description: Document the extracted audit history details in the findings file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of '/home/ga/Documents/audit_findings.txt' clearly show the original value (195), current value (125), username (mrivera), and reason (transcription error).

task_completion.description: Administratively lock the DM-101 Baseline Assessment event
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenClinica interface shows the Baseline Assessment event for DM-101 with a 'Locked' status, a padlock icon, or a success message confirming the administrative lock.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenClinica web interface to retrieve the data
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show navigation through the OpenClinica GUI in a web browser, rather than querying the database directly via terminal (e.g., psql).

integrity.description: The agent actually viewed the audit logs to get the information
integrity.visual_evidence: There is at least one screenshot showing the OpenClinica audit log interface displaying the changes, proving the agent didn't just hallucinate or copy the expected answers from the prompt.

integrity.description: The agent locked the event using the OpenClinica application
integrity.visual_evidence: The locking action was performed via the web interface (e.g., clicking 'Lock' in the event actions), not by running direct SQL UPDATE commands in the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target subject is 'DM-101'. The field being audited is 'Systolic BP'. The expected extracted data in /home/ga/Documents/audit_findings.txt must include: original value '195', current value '125', user 'mrivera', and reason containing 'transcription'. The Baseline Assessment event for DM-101 must be administratively locked via the OpenClinica GUI.
